title: [BUG] protect() throws: "Secure random number generation is not supported by this browser"
author: chenop
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

When using sheet.protect() function encounter the following error:
```
[server] This error originated either by throwing inside of an async function without a catch block, or by rejecting a promise which was not handled with .catch(). The promise rejected with the reason:
[server] Error: Secure random number generation is not supported by this browser.
[server] Use Chrome, Firefox or Internet Explorer 11
[server]     at Object.oldBrowser (/Users/chenop/MyProjects/meeba-web/node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.js:49249:9)
[server]     at Object.randomBytes (/Users/chenop/MyProjects/meeba-web/node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.js:4614:19)
[server]     at /Users/chenop/MyProjects/meeba-web/node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.js:3888:52
[server]     at new Promise (<anonymous>)
[server]     at Worksheet.protect (/Users/chenop/MyProjects/meeba-web/node_modules/exceljs/dist/exceljs.js:3878:12)
[server]     at addShiftsPerEmployeeSheets (/Users/chenop/MyProjects/meeba-web/managers/ExcelManager.js:1135:9)
```
Lib version: 4.4.0

## Steps To Reproduce

When using the following line got the exception above.
```javascript
await sheet.protect('myPass', { selectLockedCells: false, selectUnlockedCells: false });
```

## The expected behaviour:

no error is expected

node: v18.19.1
